title: Short Code Registry
description: Build a registry that maps short codes to their full values

parts.title: Register and retrieve codes
parts.description: A system uses short codes as aliases for longer values — like a URL shortener's lookup layer or a configuration key store. Implement buildRegistry(entries) / build_registry(entries) that takes an array of [code, value] pairs and returns an object (or dictionary) mapping each code to its value. If a single code appears more than once in the entries array, only the first occurrence is kept — subsequent duplicates are ignored. Also implement lookupCode(registry, code) / lookup_code(registry, code) that takes a registry object and a code string, and returns the corresponding value, or null (None in Python) if the code is not in the registry.
activeTests: builds registry from multiple entries
returns empty object for no entries
first occurrence wins for duplicate codes
single entry produces single mapping
retrieves value for registered code
returns null for unregistered code
retrieves from registry with many entries
handles code not in larger registry
retrieves empty string value for registered code

parts.title: Batch import with conflict detection
parts.description: A second service needs to import codes in batches. Implement bulkRegister(registry, entries) / bulk_register(registry, entries) that takes an existing registry (a plain object or dictionary mapping codes to values) and an array of [code, value] pairs. For each entry, if the code does not yet exist in the registry, it is included in the result. If the code already exists, the existing value is preserved and the code is recorded as a conflict. A single code may appear more than once in the entries array; only the first new occurrence is added, and subsequent duplicates are treated as conflicts. Return an object (or dictionary) with two properties: registry (the merged result containing all original entries plus new non-conflicting entries) and conflicts (an array or list of codes that were skipped, in the order they were encountered in the entries array).
